spec_match.rationale: Headline this feature API surfaces from the spec summary (no obvious gaps) are largely absent on the page; coverage is mostly topical.

interactive.rationale: Some scripted state () but no user-driven control flow — borderline static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: This release-level aggregate now links concrete ChromeStatus entries for representative Chrome 145 features covered by the tour.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on v145-tour
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for this feature introduces secondary API members but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full.

openQuestions.title: Add a user-driven control to v145-tour
openQuestions.detail: Scripted page state exists but the user can't drive the change. Add at least one button / range / select that invokes the API in-page, and re-render the result so the reader can see input → output.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on v145-tour
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: v145-tour now carries real ChromeStatus anchors for the aggregate release content. Remaining work is to make the page more complete and user-driven, as captured by the moderate follow-ups.
